title: The Waiting Soul.
content:
Breathe from the gentle south, O Lord,
    And cheer me from the north;
    Blow on the treasures of thy word,
    And call the spices forth!


    I wish, thou know�st, to be resign�d,
    And wait with patient hope;
    But hope delay�d fatigues the mind,
    And drinks the spirit up.


    Help me to reach the distant goal,
    Confirm my feeble knee;
    Pity the sickness of a soul
    That faints for love of thee.


    Cold as I feel this heart of mine,
    Yet, since I feel it so,
    It yields some hope of life divine
    Within, however low.


    I seem forsaken and alone,
    I hear the lion roar;
    And ev�ry door is shut but one,
    And that is mercy�s door.


    There, till the dear Deliv�rer come,
    I�ll wait with humble pray�r;
    An when he calls his exile home,
    The Lord shall find me there.
meaning:
This evocative piece by William Cowper, titled "The Waiting Soul.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
